title: AI Commercial Sprint
summary: A fast path from brief to polished 30-60 second ad with one studio, one model hub, voice, edit, finish, and upload.
best_for: Launch videos, product promos, UGC-style ads, and short brand films where speed matters more than bespoke tooling.
constraints: Keep one human review gate before publish.
Download generated assets before vendor URLs expire.

steps.intent: Turn the brief into scenes, shot goals, and edit notes.
Generate hero shots and alternates with direct video models plus a hub for gaps.
Record voiceover and temp score without leaving the agent session.
Assemble, caption, and revise the timeline in an agent-operable editor.
Upscale stills or plates and QC the final master.
Upload the final cut and retain metadata control.

title: Model Shootout
summary: A controlled way to test the same shot across competing video and image hubs before committing a production budget.
best_for: Directors, creative technologists, and agencies comparing Veo, Kling, Seedance, Runway, FLUX and other fast-moving models for a specific film look.
constraints: Keep prompt, input frame, model, settings, cost and output URL with every take.
Cap batch counts before spending credits.
Verify provider rights and ToS before commercial use, especially through aggregator routes.

steps.intent: Define the shot brief, constraints, and keeper criteria before touching model credits.
Create or refine the shared start frame and style references that every model will receive.
Run the same test across broad model hubs and direct endpoints, then capture task IDs and costs.
Assemble a comparison reel or contact sheet so humans can pick winners quickly.
Upscale or clean only the winning takes, not every experiment.

title: Local Edit Bay
summary: A workstation-first stack for agents that need to inspect footage, cut precisely, caption, export, and keep media local.
best_for: Editors who want agent help without sending every source file to a hosted generation or editing service.
constraints: Requires local apps and media access.
Prefer explicit file paths and review every destructive edit.
Use hosted generation only for assets that need it.

steps.intent: Index source material and pull searchable notes before cutting.
Use the right edit surface: Palmier for agent-native timeline work, Resolve for mature finishing, FFmpeg for batch operations.
Clean and normalize dialogue before final assembly.
Upscale, enhance, and verify deliverables locally or through focused APIs.
Send review copies, archive outputs, and publish only after approval.

title: Character-Consistent Series
summary: A stack for recurring characters, repeatable looks, and short episodic work where continuity beats raw novelty.
best_for: Serial social films, AI influencers, explainers, recurring product mascots, and episodic shorts.
constraints: Keep reference images and approved takes organized.
Record model, prompt, seed, and rights notes per shot.
Use one source of truth for character state.

steps.intent: Track characters, locations, and shot state so each session starts grounded.
Create locked references, style frames, logos, and art direction boards.
Generate scenes with character-aware tools and a full-stack studio for continuity.
Keep voice and dialogue consistent across episodes.
Assemble episodes and preserve reusable project structure.
Publish and read performance signals for the next episode.

title: Archive To Trailer
summary: Find the right moments in a footage library, make a trailer or cutdown, and ship reviewable versions.
best_for: Dailies, long interviews, b-roll libraries, event footage, and generated-clip archives that need fast retrieval.
constraints: Index only footage you are allowed to process.
Keep human review before publishing public cuts.

steps.intent: Collect footage sources and production notes.
Search semantic video moments, transcripts, and media bins before assembling.
Create clean review exports and delivery variants.
Host the cut, collect review feedback, and update metadata.

title: Open Lab
summary: A mostly local, hackable pipeline for technical filmmakers who want workflows they can inspect and modify.
best_for: R&D, local GPU shops, labs, students, open-source film experiments, and teams that need maximum control.
constraints: Pin model versions and node packs.
Budget time for render failures and dependency drift.

steps.intent: Generate stills, boards, and model tests locally or through inspectable workflows.
Build scenes, props, previz, and open video-model experiments.
Draft SFX locally and fill gaps with licensed library search.
Transcode, caption, QC, and assemble media with guardrailed local tools.
Publish when ready, with analytics available for iteration.
